text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Quant funds getting crushed on momentum unwind is the real story for you - that's exactly the playbook your book runs. China factory data beat on tech/export demand is a modest tailwind for chips, but yen at a 40-year low adds macro noise and European inflation staying hot keeps rate-cut hopes muted. Momentum names can get hit hard and fast in this environment.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one screaming for attention - it's a 3X leveraged chip ETF, decay is relentless, and the momentum unwind headline is a direct threat. Stop is $200, price is $236 - still above it, but margin amplifies every tick down. SPCX (+0.53%) is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history - treat it like it can gap 20% either way. SMH (-0.69%) is mild but worth watching alongside SOXL since they move together.

Watch: Whether the momentum unwind deepens today - if it does, SOXL and SMH get hit first. SOXL's $200 stop.

Bottom line: Protect SOXL on margin - that's your only urgent job today.
plays: SOXL: momentum unwind + 3X decay + margin = your biggest live risk. If it breaks $215 intraday, consider trimming before your $200 hard stop to reduce margin exposure now.
CRWD: already own it, scanner confirms grade-A daily uptrend, entry zone 741-742, stop $740 tight (below that, no edge). No add unless it holds above $742 cleanly.
No new buys today - quant momentum unwind is active and most scanner setups are chasing premium. Hold, protect SOXL, watch levels.
